Help me write MSTest tests for this formatting utility. I want to use the right MSTest string assertion methods like Assert.StartsWith and Assert.EndsWith to validate the output formats.

```csharp
namespace Contoso.Text;

public sealed class StringFormatter
{
    public string FormatPhoneNumber(string digits)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(digits);
        if (digits.Length != 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("Must be exactly 10 digits.", nameof(digits));
        return $"({digits[..3]}) {digits[3..6]}-{digits[6..]}";
    }

    public string BuildGreeting(string name, string title = "Mr.")
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);
        return $"Dear {title} {name},";
    }

    public string GenerateSlug(string title)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(title);
        return Regex.Replace(title.Trim().ToLowerInvariant(), @"[^a-z0-9]+", "-").Trim('-');
    }

    public string FormatCurrency(decimal amount, string symbol = "$")
    {
        return $"{symbol}{amount:F2}";
    }
}
```

This also means MSTest v4 likely has `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` too. Let me check - the user originally asked for those. Let me look at the analyzer warning more carefully:

- MSTEST0037: Use 'Assert.DoesNotStartWith' instead of 'Assert.IsFalse'

data.content: The MSTest v4 `Assert.StartsWith`/`Assert.EndsWith` parameter order is `(expectedPrefix, value)` — opposite of `StringAssert.StartsWith`. Let me fix the argument order:
data.reasoningText:
The parameter order for `Assert.StartsWith` in MSTest v4 is different from `StringAssert.StartsWith`. Looking at the error:

```
Assert.StartsWith failed. String '(555)' does not start with string '(555) 123-4567'.
```

So `Assert.StartsWith(result, "(555)")` is treating `result` as the expected prefix and `"(555)"` as the value. The signature must be `Assert.StartsWith(string value, string expectedPrefix)` — i.e., the arguments are reversed compared to `StringAssert.StartsWith(string value, string substring)`.
